card_summary: A newspaper clipping from The Guardian (London, England), dated September 7, 2002, page 364. Published twenty years after Dick's death, this likely relates to the continued cultural reception of his work, possibly connected to a film adaptation or publishing event.
page_summary:
A scanned newspaper page from The Guardian (London, England), dated Saturday, September 7, 2002, page 364. The clipping was sourced from Newspapers.com and downloaded on April 9, 2025. The PDF text layer contains only source metadata with no recoverable article text.

The high page number (364) suggests this is from a weekend supplement or special section of The Guardian. September 2002 saw the release of Minority Report, the Steven Spielberg film based on Dick's short story, which generated extensive media coverage of Dick's life and work. The Guardian's continued engagement with Dick two decades after his death reflects the sustained British interest in his literary and philosophical significance, and the newspaper's role in bringing Dick's work to a broad educated readership.
